mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth is consistently positive but SPX 20-day returns are negative or flat over a 3-year period, or the Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor market conditions, which typically precedes a slowdown in economic activity and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is high and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is also elevated, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy environment aimed at curbing demand, which typically leads to a contraction in economic activity and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If periods of high FEDFUNDS and high CPIAUCSL consistently coincide with positive S&P 500 60-day returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of post-recession recovery, strong housing starts (HOUST) indicate renewed consumer and business confidence, driving construction activity and broader economic growth, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST exceeds 1000k units during a post-recession recovery, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing and mining output, signaling a healthy supply side of the economy that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows sustained growth but S&P 500 5-day returns are consistently negative over a 2-year period, or the Rank IC is below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates widespread job losses, leading to reduced consumer spending and a contraction in overall demand, which negatively impacts corporate revenues and S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS declines by more than 100k in a month, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is low, but industrial production (INDPRO) is declining, it suggests that monetary stimulus is insufficient to counteract underlying supply-side weaknesses or demand destruction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is below 2.0% and INDPRO declines MoM, but S&P 500 60-day returns are consistently positive over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic expansion, accelerating GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates robust overall economic activity, which fuels corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 YoY growth exceeds 3.0% during an expansion, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 40% of such triggered instances.
